text:
In the lush woodlands of Eldoria lived a wise old owl named Orion. Orion had seen many winters pass and countless feathers flutter away, but he never lost his keen wisdom.

One crisp autumn eve, Orion heard the distant lament of an orphaned sparrow. Its chirps were soft, hollow, and tinged with sadness. Intrigued, Orion decided to investigate. Walking through the fallen leaves, Orion discovered the sparrow playing a solitary tune beside an old oak.

Orion decided to teach it a new song. He began to sing a cheerful melody that told tales of love and friendship. As the sparrow sang back, their voices joined in harmony. Soon, a flock of sparrows joined them, and together they made a symphony of songs.

Orion stayed quiet until the moonlight was high. Then, he gently took flight, singing to every bird that would listen. In the woodlands around Eldoria, birds fell silent to listen; even the night foxes paused
